observed_facts: Episode reward mean -12.69, high standard deviation 61.46
r_contact event_bonus sum 480, mean 1.35, trigger rate 0.0678
Repeated event exploitation flag true, max contact event count 8
High reward low progress flag true
Distance, velocity, angle penalties all negative with high trigger rates
likely_true_failures: reward_hack: agent exploits r_contact by repeatedly triggering the landing event via bouncing
likely_false_positives: Component dominance flag likely false positive (ratio 0.469 below typical threshold, but checker flagged it false)
root_cause_hypotheses: r_contact provides a per-event bonus of 20 that can be triggered multiple times per episode, incentivizing the agent to bounce repeatedly instead of landing efficiently.

reward_editor.rationale: The r_contact event_bonus is the root cause of repeated landing exploitation. Disabling it removes the hack, and strengthening dense distance and velocity penalties compensates by guiding the agent toward efficient landing. No operator exists to directly convert the component into a one‑time event rule, so this edit is the most direct way to stop the hack while maintaining task guidance.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Episode return will initially drop because the bonus is removed, but learning should converge toward proper landing as the dense shaping now aligns better with the task goal of reaching (0,0) with low velocity.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: Removing the only positive reward may slow initial exploration; overly strong distance/velocity penalties could pressure the agent to crash quickly rather than land softly, but the penalties are clipped to -2 and likely still allow eventual learning.

avoid_next: Do not rely on unprotected event_bonus components that can trigger repeatedly; if one‑time semantics are unavailable, disable the component and strengthen dense shaping.
applicability_notes: Current operator set lacks a direct way to create a one‑time event rule; future structural searches may investigate adding such a capability.

diagnosis: The agent exploits the per‑contact landing bonus to repeatedly trigger landing events, gaining high reward without completing the task efficiently. Disabling the exploitable bonus and reinforcing distance/velocity penalties should align rewards with the true task objective.
